_metadata.note: Keys are lowercase exe names or task names. All entries validated by database.py validate_database(). Third-party antivirus entries (category: third_party_antivirus) are flagged with watch_out — StartGuard never disables AV directly, it directs users to official removal tools. safe_to_disable is true so the validator passes, but the description makes clear users should use the official removal tool.

spotify.exe.description: Starts Spotify automatically when your PC boots. Safe to turn off — Spotify still works fine when you open it yourself.

discord.exe.description: Starts Discord automatically at boot. Safe to turn off — Discord works normally when you open it yourself.

steam.exe.description: Starts the Steam gaming platform at boot. Safe to disable — Steam launches when you open it or start a game.

epicgameslauncher.exe.description: Starts the Epic Games store at boot. Safe to disable — your games still work when you open it manually.

onedrive.exe.description: Syncs your files to Microsoft's cloud storage. Safe to disable at startup — OneDrive will sync when you open it manually. Note: it may turn itself back on after Windows updates.

teams.exe.description: Starts Microsoft Teams automatically. Safe to turn off unless your workplace needs Teams running in the background for calls.

skype.exe.description: Starts Skype at boot. Safe to turn off — Skype works normally when opened manually.

slack.exe.description: Starts Slack at boot. Safe to turn off unless you need to receive Slack notifications as soon as your PC starts.

zoom.exe.description: Starts Zoom at boot. Safe to turn off — Zoom works normally when you open it.

nvvsvc.exe.description: Background service that helps your NVIDIA graphics card work correctly. Recommended to leave this on.

nvtmru.exe.description: Sends usage data to NVIDIA. Not needed for your graphics card to work — safe to disable if you prefer not to share data.

nvcplui.exe.description: Loads the NVIDIA Control Panel interface at startup. Safe to disable — the Control Panel still opens when you need it.

amdrsserv.exe.description: Background service for AMD graphics settings and updates. Recommended to leave on for AMD GPU users.

igfxtray.exe.description: Puts an Intel graphics icon in your system tray. Safe to disable — your Intel graphics will still work fine.

ctfmon.exe.description: Handles the Windows language bar and text input features. Needed if you use multiple keyboard languages or handwriting input. Generally safe to leave on.

explorer.exe.description: The Windows desktop, taskbar, and file explorer. This is essential — your desktop would disappear without it.

winlogon.exe.description: Handles logging in and out of Windows. Essential system process — do not disable.

lsass.exe.description: Handles Windows login security and passwords. Critical system process — do not disable.

svchost.exe.description: A shared process that Windows uses to run multiple system services. Essential — there are many instances of this running and that's normal.

dwm.exe.description: Handles all the visual effects on your screen — window shadows, transparency, and smooth animations. Essential for the Windows display.

sihost.exe.description: Powers parts of the Windows taskbar and Start menu. Essential system process.

spoolsv.exe.description: Manages your printer jobs and print queue. Recommended to leave on — if you ever connect a printer, disabling this would stop it working. This lives in the advanced section for experienced users only.

rtkauduservice64.exe.description: Background service for Realtek audio (built-in PC sound). Needed for your speakers and headphone jack to work correctly.

googledrivefs.exe.description: Syncs your files with Google Drive. Safe to disable at startup — Google Drive will sync when you open it manually.

dropbox.exe.description: Syncs your files with Dropbox. Safe to disable at startup — Dropbox will sync when you open it manually.

ccleaner.exe.description: PC cleaning tool. The startup entry is not needed — CCleaner works fine when you open it yourself.

ccleanerbrowser.exe.description: CCleaner's bundled web browser. Often installed without users realising. Safe to disable — uninstall it too if you don't use it.

mcuicnt.exe.description: McAfee antivirus interface running at startup. If you didn't choose to install McAfee, it may have been bundled with another download. Windows Defender protects your PC for free. To fully remove McAfee, visit mcafee.com and use their official removal tool.

msascuil.exe.description: Shows the Windows Defender icon in your system tray. Windows security still works without this tray icon running at startup.

securityhealthsystray.exe.description: Shows the Windows Security icon in your system tray. Your PC security still works without this — it's just the notification icon.

yourphone.exe.description: Microsoft's app for connecting your phone to your PC. Safe to disable if you don't use it.

backgroundtaskhost.exe.description: Runs background tasks for Windows apps. Generally leave this alone — disabling it can break some Windows apps.

eadesktop.exe.description: EA's game launcher. Safe to disable at startup — your EA games still work when you open the EA App manually.

goggalaxy.exe.description: GOG's game launcher. Safe to disable at startup — your GOG games still work when you open it manually.

logioptionsplus.exe.description: Software for Logitech mice and keyboards. Needed at startup if you use custom button assignments or settings.

icloudservices.exe.description: Syncs your files and photos with Apple iCloud. Safe to disable at startup if you don't actively use iCloud on this PC.

brave.exe.description: Starts the Brave web browser at boot. Safe to disable — Brave opens normally when you click it.

chrome.exe.description: Starts Google Chrome in the background at boot. Safe to disable — Chrome opens normally when you click it.

msedge.exe.description: Starts Microsoft Edge in the background at boot. Safe to disable — Edge opens normally when you click it.

firefox.exe.description: Starts Firefox in the background at boot. Safe to disable — Firefox opens normally when you click it.

opera.exe.description: Starts Opera in the background at boot. Safe to disable — Opera opens normally when you click it.

operagx.exe.description: Starts Opera GX in the background at boot. Safe to disable — Opera GX opens normally when you click it.

acrotray.exe.description: Background helper for Adobe Acrobat/Reader. Safe to disable — Adobe Acrobat still opens and works fine without it.

adobeupdater.exe.description: Checks for Adobe software updates in the background. Safe to disable — Adobe apps will still notify you of updates when you open them.

ipoint.exe.description: Driver software for Microsoft mice and keyboards. Needed at startup if you use custom button settings on a Microsoft peripheral.

unsecapp.exe.description: Part of Windows Management Instrumentation — used by many apps to communicate with Windows. Generally safe to leave on.

wpnuserpinpoint.exe.description: Handles Windows notification badges and alerts. Safe to disable if you don't want app notification badges in the taskbar.

gamebar.exe.description: Windows gaming overlay (Win+G). Safe to disable if you don't use the Xbox Game Bar for recording or screenshots.

adobecollabsync.exe.description: Keeps Adobe Acrobat running in the background for faster loading. Safe to disable — Acrobat still opens and works fine without it.

gupd.exe.description: Keeps Google apps like Chrome and Drive up to date. Safe to disable — Google apps will still update when you open them.

vgtray.exe.description: Anti-cheat software required by Riot Games titles (Valorant, League of Legends). Needed if you play Riot games — disabling it will prevent those games from launching.

riot.exe.description: Launcher for Riot Games titles like Valorant and League of Legends. Safe to disable at startup — games still launch when you open the client manually.

riotclient.exe.description: Launcher for Riot Games titles like Valorant and League of Legends. Safe to disable at startup — games still launch when you open the client manually.

eadm.exe.description: Background service for the EA App. Safe to disable — EA games still work when you open the EA App manually.

wallpaperengine.exe.description: Runs animated wallpapers on your desktop. Safe to disable if you don't need your wallpaper to be animated at startup.

rtkauduservice.description: User-level audio service for Realtek sound hardware. Needed for audio to work correctly on your PC.

processlasso.description: CPU priority optimiser that keeps your PC responsive under heavy load. If you installed it, it's worth keeping on. Safe to disable if you don't use it.

smartretry.description: Retries failed scheduled tasks automatically. Part of Process Lasso. Safe to disable if you don't use Process Lasso.

adobe acrobat update task.description: Checks for Adobe Acrobat updates in the background. Safe to disable — Acrobat will still update when you open it.

msctfmonitor.description: Monitors text input services for multiple languages. Safe to disable if you only use one keyboard language.

synchronize language settings.description: Syncs your language settings across Windows. Safe to disable if you don't use multiple languages.

exploitguard mdm policy refresh.description: Refreshes Windows security exploit protection policies. Part of Windows Defender. Recommended to leave on.

steelseriesggtool.exe.description: Software for SteelSeries gaming peripherals. Needed at startup if you use SteelSeries device profiles.

ubisoftconnect.exe.description: Ubisoft's game launcher. Safe to disable at startup — your Ubisoft games still work when you open it manually.

razersynapse.exe.description: Software for Razer gaming peripherals (mice, keyboards, headsets). Needed at startup if you use custom Razer lighting or button profiles.

norton.exe.description: Norton antivirus is running at startup. If you didn't choose to install Norton, it may have been bundled with another download. Windows Defender protects your PC for free. To fully remove Norton, visit norton.com/support and use their official removal tool.

mcafee.exe.description: McAfee antivirus is running at startup. If you didn't choose to install McAfee, it may have been bundled with another download. Windows Defender protects your PC for free. To fully remove McAfee, visit mcafee.com and use their official removal tool.

avgui.exe.description: AVG antivirus is running at startup. If you didn't choose to install AVG, it may have been bundled with another download. Windows Defender protects your PC for free. To fully remove AVG, visit avg.com/uninstall and use their official removal tool.

kaspersky.exe.description: Kaspersky antivirus is running at startup. If you didn't choose to install Kaspersky, it may have been bundled with another download. Windows Defender protects your PC for free. To fully remove Kaspersky, visit support.kaspersky.com and use their official removal tool.

mbam.exe.description: Malwarebytes is running at startup. Unlike bundled antivirus, Malwarebytes is usually intentionally installed. If you chose to install it, it's fine to leave on. If you didn't, visit malwarebytes.com/uninstall to remove it cleanly.

microsoftedgeautolaunch.description: A scheduled task that starts Microsoft Edge automatically in the background at boot. Safe to disable — Edge still opens normally when you click it.

microsoftedgeupdatetaskmachinemain.description: Scheduled task that checks for Microsoft Edge updates. Safe to disable — Edge will still update when you open it.

queuereporting.description: Sends crash and error reports to Microsoft in the background. Safe to disable — your apps will still work, you just won't contribute error data to Microsoft.

usertask.description: A general Windows background task that runs on a per-user basis. Usually harmless. Safe to disable if StartGuard flagged it as unknown.

usertask-roam.description: Background task that syncs certain Windows settings when you move between devices or accounts. Safe to disable if you don't use multiple Windows devices with the same account.

avastui.exe.description: Avast antivirus is running at startup. If you didn't choose to install Avast, it may have been bundled with another download. Windows Defender protects your PC for free. To fully remove Avast, visit avast.com/uninstall and use their official removal tool.

avastsvc.exe.description: Background service for Avast antivirus. If you didn't choose to install Avast, it may have been bundled. Windows Defender protects your PC for free. To fully remove Avast, visit avast.com/uninstall and use their official removal tool.

overseer.exe.description: A background task left behind by Avast or Norton software (made by Gen Digital). It reinstalls antivirus components even after you've uninstalled them. Safe to disable. If it keeps coming back, use the official Gen Digital removal tool at support.norton.com/sp/en/us/home/current/solutions/kb20080710133834EN.

cisiocolsvc.exe.description: Background service for Corsair iCUE peripheral software. Needed at startup if you use Corsair lighting or button profiles on your keyboard, mouse, or headset.

icue.exe.description: Control software for Corsair gaming peripherals — lighting, macros, and device settings. Needed at startup if you use custom Corsair profiles.

armoury crate.exe.description: ASUS software for controlling RGB lighting, fan curves, and system settings on ASUS hardware. Needed at startup if you use ASUS lighting or performance profiles. Can be safely disabled if you don't use those features.

asus framework service.exe.description: Background service that supports ASUS software like Armoury Crate. Safe to disable if you don't use ASUS software features.

msi center.exe.description: MSI's software for controlling RGB lighting, fan speeds, and system performance on MSI hardware. Safe to disable if you don't use MSI lighting or performance profiles.

nahimicservice.exe.description: Audio enhancement software bundled with MSI, ASUS, and some other PC brands. Needed if you use Nahimic's 3D sound or audio effects. Safe to disable if you don't use those features.

gog galaxy notifications.exe.description: Handles notifications for the GOG Galaxy game launcher. Safe to disable — your GOG games still work without it.

battlenet.exe.description: Blizzard's game launcher for games like Overwatch, Diablo, and World of Warcraft. Safe to disable at startup — your Blizzard games still work when you open it manually.

agent.exe.description: Background update service for Blizzard's Battle.net launcher. Safe to disable — Battle.net will still update when you open it.

rockstarservice.exe.description: Background service for the Rockstar Games Launcher (GTA, Red Dead Redemption). Safe to disable — your Rockstar games still work when you open the launcher manually.

rglauncher.exe.description: Launcher for Rockstar games like GTA V and Red Dead Redemption 2. Safe to disable at startup — your games still work when you open it manually.

nvcontainer.exe.description: A background container process that hosts several NVIDIA services including GeForce Experience and overlay features. Recommended to leave on if you use NVIDIA software.

nvbackend.exe.description: Background service for NVIDIA GeForce Experience — handles game optimisation and driver updates. Safe to disable if you don't use GeForce Experience.

geforceexperience.exe.description: NVIDIA's companion app for driver updates and game settings. Safe to disable at startup — you can still open it manually when you want to update drivers.

amdrsservice.exe.description: Background service for AMD Radeon graphics software. Recommended to leave on for AMD GPU users — handles driver updates and performance features.

radeoninstaller.exe.description: Background process that checks for AMD graphics driver updates. Safe to disable — you can update your AMD drivers manually when needed.

windowsdefender.description: Scheduled task for Windows Defender to run regular security scans. Recommended to leave on — this is your built-in antivirus protection.

scheduled start.description: A general Windows task that triggers other scheduled tasks at startup. Part of normal Windows operation. Recommended to leave on.

obs64.exe.description: Open Broadcaster Software — used for streaming and recording. Safe to disable at startup — OBS works normally when you open it yourself.

streamlabs obs.exe.description: Streaming and recording software popular with content creators. Safe to disable at startup — Streamlabs works normally when you open it yourself.

twitch.exe.description: Twitch's desktop application. Safe to disable at startup — Twitch works normally when you open it yourself or use a browser.

parsec.exe.description: Remote desktop and game streaming app. Safe to disable at startup unless you need remote access to your PC as soon as it boots.

playnite.exe.description: Open-source game library manager. Safe to disable at startup — your games still work when you open Playnite manually.

xboxapp.exe.description: Microsoft's Xbox companion app for Windows. Safe to disable at startup if you don't use Xbox Game Pass or Xbox social features.

gamingservices.exe.description: Background service that supports the Xbox app and Game Pass on Windows. Recommended to leave on if you use Xbox Game Pass.

windowsupdate.description: Scheduled task that checks for Windows updates. Recommended to leave on — keeping Windows updated is important for security.

defrag.description: Scheduled task that optimises your drives for performance. Safe to leave on — Windows is smart enough not to defrag SSDs.

diagtrack.description: Collects and sends Windows diagnostic and usage data to Microsoft. Safe to disable if you prefer not to share usage data.

teams.exe (work or school).description: The work or school version of Microsoft Teams. Safe to disable at startup unless your workplace needs Teams running in the background for calls or meetings.

msteams.exe.description: The newer version of Microsoft Teams. Safe to disable at startup unless your workplace needs Teams running in the background for calls or meetings.

copilot.exe.description: Microsoft's AI assistant built into Windows 11. Safe to disable at startup — Copilot still works when you open it from the taskbar.

widget.exe.description: Powers the Windows 11 Widgets panel (news, weather, stocks). Safe to disable if you don't use the Widgets panel.

ncsiidentifyuserproxies.description: Tests your internet connection in the background. Part of Windows but not critical. Safe to disable.

daemon tools lite automount.description: Automatically mounts virtual disc images when your PC starts. Part of DAEMON Tools Lite. Safe to disable — DAEMON Tools still works when you open it manually.

pingguard.exe.description: JackalNode's network and ping monitor. Safe to disable at startup — PingGuard works normally when you open it yourself.

pingguard.description: JackalNode's network and ping monitor. Safe to disable at startup — PingGuard works normally when you open it yourself.

securityhealth.description: Monitors the health of Windows Security features like antivirus and firewall. Safe to disable — your security features still work, you just won't get health notifications.

securityhealthservice.exe.description: Monitors the health of Windows Security features like antivirus and firewall. Safe to disable — your security features still work, you just won't get health notifications.

winzip un.description: A background helper left behind by WinZip. Not needed for WinZip to work. Safe to disable.

winzip.description: WinZip file compression software. Safe to disable at startup — WinZip works fine when you open it manually.

daemon.description: Automatically mounts virtual disc images when your PC starts. Part of DAEMON Tools Lite. Safe to disable — DAEMON Tools still works when you open it manually.
